VoiceR Saved Report

This is an automatically generated report by VoiceR.

Statistics Table

Normality Plots

Duration

A Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for the duration measure, W(20) = 0.98, p = 0.93.

Voice Breaks Percentage

A Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for the voice breaks percentage measure, W(20) = 0.94, p = 0.28.

Root Mean Square (RMS) of Amplitude envelope

A Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for the RMS of Amplitude envelope measure, W(20) = 0.93, p = 0.17. Although, a Shapiro-Wilk test showed a significant departure from normality for Sad condition, W(10) = 0.82, p < .05.

Loudness

A Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for the loudness measure, W(20) = 0.93, p = 0.17. Although, a Shapiro-Wilk test showed a significant departure from normality for Sad condition, W(10) = 0.79, p < .05.

Mean Fundamental Frequency (F0)

A Shapiro-Wilk test showed a significant departure from normality for the average pitch measure, W(20) = 0.8, p < .01. Although, a Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for Happy condition, W(10) = 0.92, p = 0.36.

Standard Deviation (sd) of F0

A Shapiro-Wilk test showed a significant departure from normality for the pitch’s standard deviation measure, W(20) = 0.79, p < .01.

Average Wiener Entropy

A Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for the average Wiener entropy measure, W(20) = 0.95, p = 0.39.

Average Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio

A Shapiro-Wilk test did not show a significant departure from normality for the average Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio measure, W(20) = 0.96, p = 0.54.

Comparison Plots

Duration

A T-Test did not show significant differences in duration between Happy condition (Mean = 1.06, SD = 0.15) and Sad condition (Mean = 1.05, SD = 1.05), Z = -0.2, p = 0.84

Voice Breaks Percentage

A T-Test did not show significant differences in voice breaks percentage between Happy condition (Mean = 0.46, SD = 0.23) and Sad condition (Mean = 0.31, SD = 0.31), Z = -1.36, p = 0.17

RMS of the amplitude envelope

A Wilcoxon Signed-Ranks Test indicated that RMS of the amplitude envelope mean rank was significantly higher for Happy condition (Mean Rank = 13.7) than Sad condition (Mean Rank = 7.3), Z = -2.44, p < .05

Loudness

A Wilcoxon Signed-Ranks Test indicated that loudness mean rank was significantly higher for Happy condition (Mean Rank = 13.6) than Sad condition (Mean Rank = 7.4), Z = -2.35, p < .05

Average Pitch

A Wilcoxon Signed-Ranks Test did not show significant differences between Happy condition (Mean Rank = 12.8) and Sad condition (Mean Rank = 8.2), Z = -1.42, p = 0.16

SD Pitch

A Wilcoxon Signed-Ranks Test did not show significant differences between Happy condition (Mean Rank = 11.3) and Sad condition (Mean Rank = 9.7), Z = -0.2, p = 0.84

Average Entropy

A T-Test did not show significant differences in average Entropy between Happy condition (Mean = 0.09, SD = 0.02) and Sad condition (Mean = 0.1, SD = 0.1), Z = -1.45, p = 0.15

Average Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio

A T-Test indicated that average Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio was significantly lower for Happy condition (Mean = 4.26, SD = 1.5) than Sad condition (Mean = 6.43, SD = 6.43), Z = -2.71, p < .01
